title: Wave-1: auth-seed PROBE spec (gates Waves 2/3 execution)
acceptance_criteria: Runs AFTER the full cold dev-server compile (no special ordering tricks — relies on webServer readiness).

Seeds a user via auth-seed.ts (T-04), applies authStorageState, then asserts: (i) the running server reports auth provider === 'nextauth' (via the health/getFeatureStatus endpoint — locate it; do not hard-code a guess), (ii) getCurrentUser() resolves through a REAL protected route (e.g. /{locale}/dashboard renders the authenticated view, not a redirect to /login), (iii) a real DB write+read round-trips through the running server (a route that performs a Prisma query touching the seeded user succeeds).

Failure classification: if the probe error is a PrismaClientInitializationError (error CLASS — not a message-substring match) it is a HARD STOP — the test must fail with a clear 'Wave-1 PROBE: provider/datasource mismatch — STOP, surface to user' message and not retry. Any other non-2xx is an ordinary DIRECT failure routed through normal triage.
Tagged @critical. Self-contained seed + afterAll cleanup (own dedicated user, not a global one).
npm run typecheck passes; `npx playwright test --list` includes it.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of why a Prisma-init error must hard-stop rather than retry
